ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza food product class; no specific white pizza or Zingara variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific white pizza subtype code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for white pizza or Zingara pizza variant identified in published SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Zingara White Pizza is a whole pizza of approximately 300 g, as commonly served at Italian restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat and vegetables, regular crust (SR Legacy ID 21299) and Pizza, cheese, regular crust (SR Legacy ID 21272), used as composite estimation basis for white pizza with salami, mozzarella, mushrooms, and capsicum
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for Zingara White Pizza. Values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for cheese pizza (FDCID 21272) and meat-and-vegetable pizza (FDCID 21299), adjusted to reflect white pizza preparation (no tomato sauce, higher cheese content, salami, mushrooms, capsicum). Energy, macros, and secondary nutrients are weighted averages with upward adjustment for fat and protein due to salami and extra mozzarella. Sodium is elevated to reflect cured salami content. Values represent a reasonable per-100g estimate for this pizza style.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Pizza, cheese, thin and crispy crust (GI ~50, referenced against glucose); also University of Sydney GI Database entry for pizza, cheese.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for Zingara White Pizza. The GI of 50 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 for cheese pizza (thin/regular crust), which is the closest analogous food. White pizza shares the same dough base and cheese topping profile; absence of tomato sauce does not materially alter GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 x 88.5) / 100 = 44.3, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 29.5 x 3 = 88.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from salami and mozzarella cheese
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Zingara White Pizza due to its high sodium content of approximately 680 mg per 100 g (approximately 2040 mg per whole pizza), which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index and high glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the glycemic index of white pizza is moderate-to-low at approximately 50, the high glycemic load per serving (approximately 44 GL units for a whole pizza) means that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume small portions and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat and sodium from salami and cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Zingara White Pizza contains approximately 5.2 g saturated fat and 680 mg sodium per 100 g, primarily from mozzarella cheese and cured salami; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and choose lower-fat, lower-sodium alternatives when possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Zingara White Pizza is an Italian-style pizza prepared without tomato sauce, instead featuring a base of olive oil and mozzarella cheese topped with mushrooms, salami, and capsicum (bell pepper), then baked in a high-temperature oven until the crust is golden and the cheese is melted and lightly browned. The name 'Zingara' (Italian for 'gypsy woman') is a traditional Italian culinary term applied to dishes featuring a colourful combination of vegetables and cured meats. The pizza has a rich, savoury flavour profile with the saltiness of salami, the earthiness of mushrooms, the mild sweetness of capsicum, and the creamy, slightly elastic texture of melted mozzarella on a crisp yet chewy dough base. In Singapore, this pizza is served at Italian restaurants such as Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore and is typically offered as a whole pizza. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy food providing approximately 266 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from cheese and salami (11.8 g/100 g), moderate fat (11.5 g/100 g, of which 5.2 g is saturated), and carbohydrate from the dough base (29.5 g/100 g). Sodium content is elevated at approximately 680 mg per 100 g due to the cured salami and cheese, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 50 (low-to-moderate), but the glycemic load per whole pizza serving is high, requiring portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or dairy.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Zingara White Pizza exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for cheese pizza and meat-and-vegetable pizza, adjusted for white pizza characteristics. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (cheese pizza) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the ingredient profile is well-defined and the USDA composite approach is methodologically sound.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used for this estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used for this estimation. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used for this estimation. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B vitamins, folate, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and water are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite data and should be treated as approximate. The GI of 50 is an analogous estimate from cheese pizza data in the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for Zingara White Pizza is available in any approved source. Glycemic load per serving is calculated based on a 300 g whole-pizza serving containing approximately 88.5 g carbohydrate: GL = (50 x 88.5) / 100 = 44.3. The food is served at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore, Boat Quay, Singapore, as recorded in field survey data. Price as listed on field survey data: SGD 29.32. This is an Italian cuisine dish; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply. No Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi names are required or applicable for this Italian dish.
